title: Creating a charge
text: Use the Charges endpoint to collect a one-time payment from a customer. Submit the amount in the smallest currency unit, a currency code, and a payment source token. A successful call returns a charge object with a captured status.

title: Refunding a payment
text: The Refunds resource reverses a previously completed charge, returning funds to the cardholder. You may refund the full amount or issue a partial refund by specifying a smaller amount. Refunds settle back to the original payment method.

title: Managing subscriptions
text: Subscriptions bill a customer on a recurring schedule tied to a price and a billing interval. Creating a subscription immediately starts the billing cycle and generates the first invoice. Update the plan to change what the customer is charged going forward.

title: Receiving webhook events
text: Webhooks deliver event notifications to your server whenever objects change, such as a payment succeeding or a subscription renewing. Register an endpoint URL and PayFlow will POST a signed event payload to it. Always return a 2xx status quickly to acknowledge receipt.

title: Responding to disputes
text: A dispute is opened when a cardholder contests a charge with their bank, also known as a chargeback. Submit evidence such as receipts and shipping proof before the deadline to contest it. If you lose, the disputed amount and a fee are debited from your balance.

title: Payouts to your bank
text: Payouts move your available balance from PayFlow to your connected bank account. By default payouts run on a daily rolling schedule, but you can switch to weekly or monthly. Each payout groups the underlying transactions it settles.

title: Storing customer records
text: The Customers object stores a person's contact details and saved payment methods for reuse. Attaching a customer to future charges lets you bill returning buyers without re-collecting card data. Customers also anchor subscriptions and invoices.

title: Saving payment methods
text: A payment method represents a card, bank account, or wallet a customer has authorized you to charge. Save a payment method to a customer so it can be reused for later purchases. Set one as the default to use it automatically on invoices.

title: Idempotent requests
text: Passing an idempotency key on a write request guarantees the operation runs at most once even if the request is retried after a network error. PayFlow returns the original response for any repeat of the same key. Keys expire after 24 hours.

title: Invoices and billing
text: An invoice itemizes what a customer owes for a billing period, including line items, taxes, and discounts. Invoices are created automatically for subscriptions and can also be drafted manually. Finalizing an invoice attempts payment against the default method.

title: Automatic tax calculation
text: Enable automatic tax to have PayFlow compute the correct sales tax or VAT based on the customer's address and your registered jurisdictions. The calculated tax appears as a separate line on the invoice. Keep your origin address current for accurate rates.

title: Fraud protection rules
text: The risk engine scores every payment for the likelihood of fraud and can automatically block high-risk attempts. You can add custom rules to allow, review, or reject payments based on signals like country or card testing patterns. Review flagged payments in the dashboard.

title: Marketplace split payments
text: Connect lets a platform accept a payment and route portions of it to multiple sellers. Specify a transfer to each connected account and an application fee for the platform. Funds are split at settlement time according to those instructions.

title: Supported currencies
text: PayFlow can present and settle payments in over 100 currencies. Charge a customer in their local currency while receiving settlement in your account's default currency. Conversion happens at the daily reference rate plus a small spread.

title: Smart retry for failed payments
text: When a recurring charge fails, dunning automatically retries the payment on an optimized schedule to recover revenue. Configure how many attempts to make and what to do when all retries are exhausted, such as canceling the subscription. Customers can be emailed to update their card.

title: Testing in the sandbox
text: The sandbox is an isolated test environment that mirrors production but never moves real money. Use the provided test card numbers to simulate successful charges, declines, and disputes. Test-mode API keys are prefixed to keep them separate from live keys.

title: Authenticating API requests
text: Every request must include a secret API key in the Authorization header as a bearer token. Keep secret keys on your server and never expose them in client code. Rotate a key immediately if it is ever leaked.

title: Rate limits
text: The API enforces a per-account request ceiling measured in requests per second. Exceeding it returns a 429 response with a Retry-After header indicating how long to wait. Use exponential backoff to smooth out bursts.

title: Attaching metadata
text: Metadata is a set of key-value pairs you can attach to most objects to store your own reference information, like an internal order ID. PayFlow never uses metadata for processing, but it is returned on the object and searchable in the dashboard. Limit values to 500 characters.

title: 3D Secure authentication
text: 3D Secure adds a bank-hosted verification step that shifts liability for fraudulent chargebacks to the issuer. PayFlow triggers it automatically when a card or region requires strong customer authentication. The customer completes a challenge before the payment is confirmed.

title: Hosted checkout links
text: A payment link is a shareable URL that opens a PayFlow-hosted checkout page, letting you collect payment with no frontend code. Configure the products, price, and success redirect, then send the link by email or embed it. Each completed link creates a charge and optionally a customer.

title: Discounts and coupons
text: Coupons define a percentage or fixed-amount discount that can be applied to invoices and subscriptions. Attach a coupon to a customer or subscription to reduce what they are billed, either once or for a set duration. Expired or fully redeemed coupons are rejected.

title: Proration on plan changes
text: When a subscription changes plan mid-cycle, proration credits the unused time on the old plan and charges the prorated cost of the new one. The adjustment appears as line items on the next invoice. You can disable proration to bill the full new price at renewal.

title: Sending receipts
text: PayFlow can email a branded receipt to the customer automatically after a successful payment. Provide the customer's email on the charge to trigger it, and customize the logo and colors in your branding settings. Receipts include a link to a hosted, printable copy.

title: Financial reporting exports
text: Reports let you export itemized transaction, payout, and fee data as CSV for accounting and reconciliation. Schedule recurring exports or generate them on demand for a date range. Balance reports reconcile every cent between charges, refunds, and payouts.

title: Official server SDKs
text: PayFlow publishes maintained libraries for Node, Python, Ruby, Go, PHP, and Java that wrap the REST API with typed methods. Install the package for your language and initialize it with your secret key. The SDKs handle retries, pagination, and error parsing for you.

title: Paginating list results
text: List endpoints return results in pages capped at 100 objects, ordered most recent first. Follow the cursor in the response to fetch the next page using the starting-after parameter. Iterate until the has-more flag is false to read the full set.

title: Interpreting API errors
text: Errors return a standard HTTP status plus a JSON body with a type, a machine-readable code, and a human message. A card decline surfaces a decline code explaining why the issuer refused it. Handle 4xx as client mistakes and 5xx as transient failures worth retrying.

title: Bank debit mandates
text: Charging a bank account requires a mandate, the customer's recorded authorization to debit them. Collect the mandate during setup and store its reference; some schemes require showing specific terms. Debits without a valid mandate are rejected and may be reversed.

title: Capturing an authorization
text: Placing an authorization reserves funds on a card without moving them, letting you capture the amount later once you fulfill the order. Capture within the hold window or the authorization expires and the funds are released. You may capture a lower amount than authorized.
